stories.headline: Hello Baiana Brings Brazilian Street Food to Pike Place Market
stories.body: Former Chopped and Beat Bobby Flay champion Emme Ribeiro Collins opens her Afro-Brazilian restaurant at an eight-seat counter inside Pike Place Market. The menu features dishes like moqueca, alcarajé, and rabada that often sell out at her pop-ups.
stories.location: Pike Place Market · late February/early March

stories.headline: The Notebook Musical Opens Broadway at the Paramount
stories.body: The beloved romance jumps from screen to stage in this Broadway production featuring music by Ingrid Michaelson and direction by Tony nominee Michael Greif. It's been sixteen years since the movie captivated audiences.
stories.location: Downtown · March 3–8 at The Paramount Theatre

stories.headline: The Notebook Musical Opens Hearts at Paramount
stories.body: Ryan Gosling and Rachel McAdams' beloved romance gets the Broadway treatment with songs by Ingrid Michaelson. The touring production brings Noah and Allie's love story to life through song and dance at the historic Paramount Theatre.
stories.location: Downtown · March 3–8 at The Paramount Theatre

stories.headline: Balkan Night Northwest Brings Southeastern European Fire
stories.body: The region's biggest Balkan music and dance celebration returns March 14 with acclaimed Albanian artists Merita Halili and Raif Hyseni. Three stages pack St. Demetrios Greek Orthodox Church with nonstop traditional music and dancing.

stories.headline: Hot Java Cool Jazz Features Local High School Talent
stories.body: Five local high school jazz bands showcase their skills at The Paramount Theatre in this annual celebration of young musical talent. The competition highlights the impressive depth of Seattle's music education programs.

stories.headline: Northwest Record Show Spins Into Seattle Center
stories.body: Collectors and music lovers browse 50 tables of vinyl records, CDs, and music memorabilia on March 8. Early admission starts at 8 AM for serious diggers, with regular admission running until 5 PM at the Armory.

stories.headline: Cafe Lolo Takes Over Iconic Capitol Hill Corner
stories.body: The farmers market pasta makers are opening their brick-and-mortar restaurant 'very soon' in the former Cook Weaver space at the bottom of the Loveless building. Expect house-milled grains and seasonal local produce.

stories.headline: US Women's Soccer Takes On Japan at Lumen Field
stories.body: The USWNT faces Japan in an international friendly on April 14, presented by U.S. Soccer. The match promises to showcase top-level women's soccer as both teams prepare for upcoming major tournaments.
